title: Implement run orchestration, retries, manifests, and recovery

commands.name: Missing run-manifest implementation
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/run-orchestration.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The focused examples failed to import the not-yet-created run-orchestration module

commands.name: Missing orchestration repository
commands.expectedFailure: All three queue, crash/retry, and cancellation cases failed because the orchestration repository did not exist

commands.name: Missing PostgreSQL orchestration boundary
commands.expectedFailure: The in-memory cases passed while the PostgreSQL case failed because PostgresRunOrchestrationRepository did not exist

commands.name: PostgreSQL queue, lease, manifest, and idempotency persistence
Seeded manifest, retry, and terminal-state properties
Undefined recovery and run-history stories
commands.expectedFailure: Both RUN-05 scenarios exposed nine undefined run-history and recovery step instances before the API, UI, and browser bindings existed

commands.name: Real API and browser recovery stories
Production session boundary for manifest comparison
commands.expectedFailure: The visible authenticated page loaded run history, but the separate Playwright request context received 401 instead of sharing the optimized-production session cookie

commands.name: Migration inventory exposed the new orchestration schema
commands.expectedFailure: The migration inventory still expected ten versions and twenty-eight PostgreSQL tables after the four orchestration tables were added

commands.name: Optimized-production run history and recovery stories

scenarios.scenario: Terminal run history distinguishes captured and missing coverage
A worker failure and completed retry keep exact configuration lineage

visualEvidence.rationale: Source-bound desktop and narrow run-history captures are pending the implementation commit.

proofs.description: Append-only execution scopes, worker leases, terminal manifests, idempotency receipts, and database guards
Canonical hashes, immutable manifests, queue and lease operations, retries, cancellation, integrity failures, and in-memory and PostgreSQL repositories
Focused manifest ordering, missing-coverage, immutability, and terminal-state examples
Seeded PROP-18, PROP-19, and PROP-22 retry, terminal-state, ordering, and hashing invariants
Queue claims, idempotency, crash/retry, cancel, manifest finalization, PostgreSQL persistence, and immutability
User-visible terminal coverage and exact retry-lineage recovery stories tagged RUN-05 and FR-037
Real API and browser assertions plus source-bound desktop, narrow, and manifest-comparison capture
Workspace-authorized run-history API that omits the raw worker lease token and returns immutable records
Direct terminal-state, checkpoint coverage, integrity-failure, manifest-hash, and retry-lineage presentation
RUN-05 browser report and screenshot artifact layout

knownLimitations: The committed run-history fixture uses controlled fictional records and reserved identities; it does not establish safety, compliance, agreement conformity, or effectiveness on real school software.
A worker that loses manifest finalization is retained as an explicit integrity failure rather than being mislabeled completed; retry requires a new linked run.
Source-bound visual capture, full verification, and clean-checkout CI are still pending, so RUN-05 remains in progress.
